topic: Sport and Society and the Role of Technology in Physical Activity and Sport
subject: pe

items.q: Which body ran the 'This Girl Can' campaign to boost women's participation in sport?
items.why: Students attribute the campaign wrongly; 'This Girl Can' is a Sport England initiative tackling barriers to female participation.

items.q: Whose philosophy is 'Olympism', emphasising sport, education and international understanding?
items.why: Students confuse key figures; Coubertin founded the modern Olympics and the ideals of Olympism.

items.q: What does the sociological term 'functionalism' suggest about sport?
items.correct: Sport helps maintain social order and shared values
items.misconception: Sport only benefits the ruling class
items.why: Students mix up theories; functionalism stresses sport's integrating role, unlike Marxism's focus on inequality.

items.q: 'This Girl Can' is which body's campaign?
items.why: It's Sport England. “UK Sport” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Olympism. “Functionalism” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Number of recommended weekly moderate-exercise minutes (UK, adults)?
items.why: Exact answer: 150 minutes. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
